puzzle.title: The Warning in the Wicked Cities
puzzle.intro: Imagine being sent with a divine message to a society lost in unprecedented wrongdoing. The people you call to righteousness are your own, yet they mock your guidance and persist in their transgressions. You stand as a lone voice of purity, warning them of a devastating end, even as heavenly visitors arrive at your door, bringing both a test and a final verdict. Can you identify this steadfast messenger?
clues: I was a contemporary and follower of a great patriarch of faith, and I migrated with him for the sake of Allah.
I was sent as a messenger to a people who committed a transgression no creation had ever committed before them.
When divine guests, in the form of handsome young men, visited my home, I was distressed, fearing for their safety from my own people.
In my desperation to protect my guests, I offered my own daughters to the mob that surrounded my house, hoping to turn them from their wicked intentions.
A member of my own household, who stayed behind, was destined to be among those who perished in the punishment.
My people were destroyed by a shower of stones of baked clay, and their towns were turned upside down as a sign for all who came after.

trial.label: What Was My Primary Mission?
options: Building a massive ark to survive a global flood
Challenging a tyrant who claimed divinity
Calling people to stop fraudulent business practices
Warning a people against unprecedented immorality
Demanding the release of an enslaved people
trial.answer: Warning a people against unprecedented immorality

location.label: What Was the Nature of the Punishment?
options: A shower of stones of baked clay
location.answer: A shower of stones of baked clay

outcome.label: Who Was Left Behind to Perish?

puzzle.arabic: قَالَ إِنَّ فِيهَا لُوطًۭا ۚ قَالُوا۟ نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن فِيهَا ۖ لَنُنَجِّيَنَّهُۥ وَأَهْلَهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَهُۥ كَانَتْ مِنَ ٱلْغَـٰبِرِينَ
puzzle.verse: [Abraham] said, "Indeed, within it is Lot." They said, "We are more knowing of who is within it. We will surely save him and his family, except his wife. She is to be of those who remain behind." (29:32)
